findings.description: SQL injection: the user-supplied `fragment` is interpolated via f-string directly into the SQL text (WHERE name LIKE '%{fragment}%'), allowing UNION-based data exfiltration, boolean/blind extraction, and schema enumeration via sqlite_master (CWE-89, OWASP A03 Injection); fix by parameterizing with `LIKE ?` and binding `('%'+fragment+'%',)`.

TOCTOU race in reserve(): the stock SELECT and the decrement UPDATE are not atomic, so concurrent callers can both pass the check and oversell, violating inventory integrity; fix with an atomic conditional UPDATE (... WHERE sku=? AND stock>=?) checking rowcount, or a locked transaction.

Missing input validation on `quantity` in restock()/reserve(): a negative quantity passed by a caller can corrupt stock levels (integrity), though this is a logic-class concern deferred to sibling critics.
Off-by-one in reserve(): `row["stock"] > quantity` rejects reservations that exactly equal available stock (should be >=); logic-class, noted for completeness and deferred to sibling critics.
